title: K59 Surf Resort (El Zonte)
summary: Beachfront surf hotel at K59 break, El Zonte. Direct beach access, surf instruction, Bitcoin Beach adjacent. WhatsApp: +503 7069 6976.
why_text: Go-to base for El Zonte surf corridor. Bitcoin Beach community is walkable. Strong local surf and Bitcoin scene. K59 is one of El Salvador's most consistent reef breaks.

title: Surf Farm El Salvador — Permaculture + K59 (Taquillo)
summary: Permaculture farm and surf lodge in Taquillo village, 10min from El Zonte. Mango, papaya, banana, lemon trees. Walk to K59 and K61 surf breaks. Airbnb hosting since 2015. Website: surffarmelsalvador.com.
why_text: Only working permaculture farm in the El Zonte surf corridor. Small, intimate, locals-connected. Strong offbeat + food + surf combo. Good base for a night away from San Salvador with agricultural vibes.

title: Jiquilisco Bay Full-Day Wildlife Expedition
summary: Comprehensive tour covering spider monkeys, cacao plantations, and mangrove canals in Jiquilisco Bay. WhatsApp: +503 7900 5712 (Tunco Life).
why_text: Tunco Life provides professional logistics from El Tunco/San Salvador, making this remote wildlife hotspot much easier to visit for solo travelers or small groups.

title: Spider Monkey Sanctuary at Puerto Barillas
summary: Guided walk into the Nacascol Reserve to see friendly spider monkeys in their natural habitat. WhatsApp: +503 7729 4029.
why_text: One of the few places in El Salvador where you can reliably see spider monkeys up close in a protected sanctuary within the Jiquilisco Bay mangroves.

title: Jiquilisco Bay Mangrove Birding Boat Tour
summary: Guided boat tour through the extensive mangrove canals of Jiquilisco Bay, a UNESCO RAMSAR site.
why_text: Observation of over 280 bird species, including the American Oystercatcher and Boat-billed Heron in a unique wetland environment.

title: Barra de Santiago Wetland Discovery
summary: Exploring the biological wealth of Barra de Santiago via small boat or kayak to observe aquatic and coastal birds.
why_text: Diverse coastal ecosystem offering sightings of migratory and resident birds in a less-crowded, authentic setting.

title: Cinquera Civil War & Tropical Forest Trek
summary: Historical hike led by ex-combatants through the Cinquera dry forest, visiting guerrilla camps and waterfalls. WhatsApp: +503 7600-7313 (Inter Tours).
source_hint: Inter Tours El Salvador / Cinquera ARDM
why_text: Cinquera is a powerful living history site where you hear first-hand accounts of the conflict while exploring a unique forest ecosystem.

title: Crocodile & Mangrove Tour at Barra de Santiago
summary: Boat tour through the pristine mangroves of Barra de Santiago to spot crocodiles and water birds. WhatsApp: +503 7844 4336 (Eco Tours Petate).
why_text: Barra de Santiago is a RAMSAR site with a high concentration of crocodiles and a very well-preserved mangrove system, offering an authentic wilderness experience.

title: Dawn Birdwatching at Ecoparque El Espino
summary: Early morning birding session starting at 5:30 AM to catch the peak activity of resident species.
why_text: High chance to see the Turquoise-browed Motmot (national bird) and Elegant Trogon in a protected area on the outskirts of San Salvador.

title: El Sunzal Long Right-Point Session
summary: Legendary long right point with outside/inside sections, consistent year-round surf, and workable windows for both progressing and expert surfers.
source_hint: El Salvador Travel destination page for El Sunzal
why_text: Adds a missing canonical break between Tunco and Punta Roca with strong operational detail on sections, tide behavior, and seasonal swell.

title: Rancho La Playita surf and local lunch
summary: Friendly break and home-cooked local food nearby.
why_text: High local warmth and less crowded surf energy.

title: Beef Back Better (Owen Gwilliam)
summary: Australian founder Owen Gwilliam's grass-fed, grass-finished beef operation delivering 100% Salvadoran cattle with no hormones, antibiotics, or additives. Bitcoin payments accepted. Orders via WhatsApp.
why_text: Rare quality regenerative beef in El Salvador, run by a Bitcoin-aligned Aussie expat. Strong local_people and crypto signal. Owen is an organic ag veteran (20yr experience, authored Australian organic standards) who relocated to SV in 2022. Meet him for the intersection of Bitcoin, food sovereignty, and local agriculture. Possible farm visit to see cattle on pasture.

title: Highland Birds of El Boquerón National Park
summary: Birdwatching excursion at 1,800 meters above sea level on the rim of the San Salvador Volcano crater.
why_text: Habitat for highland species such as the Bushy-crested Jay, Rufous-collared Robin, and various hummingbirds.

title: Estero de Jaltepeque Mangrove Boat Experience
summary: Scenic boat tour through the channels of the Jaltepeque Estuary, exploring the mangrove forests and sandbars. WhatsApp: +503 7460 8004.
why_text: Easily accessible from San Salvador, this estuary offers a beautiful maze of mangrove tunnels and the famous 'Bancos de Arena' sandbars.

title: Suchitoto Indigo Dyeing Workshop with Robert Broz
summary: Hands-on workshop to learn about El Salvador's 'Blue Gold' (indigo) with Robert Broz or Irma Guadrón. WhatsApp: +503 7860-9435.
why_text: Suchitoto is the heart of indigo heritage; learning from Robert or Irma provides deep historical context and a unique, personal souvenir.

title: El Tunco Surf Base + La Bocana Progression Session
summary: Use El Tunco as an easy surf base with beginner-friendly sections near the pig-shaped rock and optional advanced La Bocana checks.
source_hint: El Salvador Travel destination page for El Tunco (La Bocana + beginner sections + in-town surf logistics)
why_text: Converts a generic Tunco entry into a source-backed progression option with explicit skill-split (beginner sections vs advanced La Bocana).

title: Jiquilisco Bay mangrove kayak and sea-turtle dawn trip
summary: Quiet mangrove tunnels, shorebird flocks, turtle nesting wins.
source_hint: Search local eco operators in Jiquilisco/Usulutan
why_text: High wildlife density and low-noise nature immersion.

title: Nahuat Language & Cultural Session with Walter García
summary: Private session to learn about Pipil culture and the Nahuat language with a local expert. WhatsApp: +503 7556 3944.
why_text: Nahuat is an endangered language; a session with Walter provides rare indigenous perspective and supports cultural preservation.

title: El Zonte Point + River-Mouth + Beach-Break Combo
summary: Multi-section surf day at El Zonte covering the iconic right point plus west-side river-mouth left and faster beach-break pockets.
source_hint: El Salvador Travel destination page for El Zonte
why_text: Adds underrepresented El Zonte section diversity and clarifies that one zone can serve different wave styles in a single day.

title: Private Estuary Catamaran with Tetos Tour
summary: Private boat or catamaran rentals for exploring the Estero de Jaltepeque in comfort. WhatsApp: +503 7852 2529.
source_hint: Tetos Tour / Costa del Sol rentals
why_text: Ideal for groups looking for a more comfortable or social way to explore the mangroves and sunset views in the Jaltepeque Estuary.

title: Santa Ana Volcano Private Sunrise Hike (Tunco Life)
summary: Private early-morning hike to the crater of Santa Ana (Ilamatepec) for sunrise views, avoiding the crowds. WhatsApp: +503 6985-8984.
why_text: The standard park group departs late (11 AM); Tunco Life organizes private logistics for earlier, cooler, and less crowded summit experiences.

title: Punta Roca Advanced Right-Point Session (La Libertad)
summary: Dawn or mid-tide session at Punta Roca, a fast rocky-bottom right point reserved for advanced surfers.
source_hint: El Salvador Travel destination page for Punta Roca (break profile, seasonality, access)
why_text: Strengthens the canonical high-performance break in the KB with direct official break-season and hazard details for March-April planning.

title: Los Cobanos Marine Reserve snorkeling
summary: Protected reef fragments and clear-water windows.
source_hint: Reserve operators out of La Libertad/Acajutla
why_text: Strong fish/snorkel anchor for west-coast phase.

title: Wassi Surf Shop Lessons and Rentals (El Tunco)
summary: Book lessons, board rentals, or camp support at an active El Tunco surf school with direct phone contact and local break familiarity.
source_hint: Wassi Surf Shop official site (services + contact + Tunco break access)
why_text: Adds a current, directly contactable school option tied to Tunco/Sunzal/Punta Roca logistics for short trip execution.

title: Archeological Deep Dive with Nahuat Tours
summary: Guided exploration of Joya de Cerén (the 'Pompeii of the Americas') and San Andrés ruins with specialized guides. WhatsApp: +503 7929 6166.
why_text: Nahuat Tours specializes in archaeology; their guides provide expert level detail that casual tours often miss.

title: The Beef Initiative El Salvador (Texas Slim)
summary: U.S.-based cattle genetics and food sovereignty org that signed a cooperation agreement with El Salvador's Ministry of Agriculture (MAG) in Nov 2025 at the Bitcoin Histórico event. Led by Texas Slim (John Norden), Bitcoin-aligned rancher and founder of BeefInitiative.com.
why_text: Texas Slim is the most prominent Bitcoin+beef personality globally. He signed a formal partnership with El Salvador's government to overhaul cattle genetics nationwide. If he or his team are in-country during your trip, this is the most interesting person in the beef x Bitcoin x food sovereignty space to meet. Connect via X @modernTman.

title: Acajutla snorkel at Piedra del Mangle
summary: Half-day reef fish and easy boat runs.
source_hint: Ask hotel or local dive operators in Acajutla
why_text: Fast win for fish-focused ocean day.

title: Bitcoin Beach
summary: Locally-led Salvadoran circular-economy initiative with public bitcoin donation channel.
why_text: Supports on-the-ground Bitcoin circular economy and education in coastal communities.

title: Barra de Santiago or Jiquilisco bird and crocodile boat tour
summary: Estuarine wildlife and major wader populations.
why_text: Hits both birds and weird-awesome estuary ecology.

title: Guazapa Tours — Community Horseback Rides (Guazapa)
summary: Community-led youth ecotourism group at Cerro Guazapa offering horseback rides for $25-35/person. WhatsApp: +503 7959 3394. Local community experience near the historic Guazapa volcano.
why_text: Authentic community tourism at Cerro Guazapa — youth-led, locally owned, affordable. Strong local_people signal. $25-35/person horseback makes it budget-accessible. Historic FMLN guerrilla territory adds political/history layer. ~45min from San Salvador.

title: Hope House El Salvador
summary: Community organization in El Zonte with official bitcoin donation options (Lightning + on-chain).
why_text: Directly supports youth/community programs in the same coastal zone you plan to visit.

title: Bitcoin Beach
summary: Public contact lead in section: Crypto / Bitcoin.
why_text: Useful for booking, confirmations, and local coordination.

why_text: Useful for booking, confirmations, and local coordination.

title: Finca Chantecuan — Beekeeping, Camping & Nature (San Salvador)
summary: Beekeeping farm with camping, hiking, and nature activities near San Salvador. Phone: +503 7147 8768. Rare urban-adjacent nature escape with hands-on apiculture.
why_text: Only beekeeping farm experience found near San Salvador. Camping on-site. Strong offbeat signal — not typical tourist fare. Quick escape from the city with local ecological focus.

title: Birdwatching Tours El Salvador (Rodolfo)
summary: Public contact lead in section: Birding.
why_text: Useful for booking, confirmations, and local coordination.

title: Rancho Mi Sueño (Playa San Diego, La Libertad)
summary: Ranked #3 El Salvador ranch on Tripadvisor. Located near Playa San Diego, La Libertad — close to both surf coast and San Salvador.
why_text: Top-3 Tripadvisor ranch on the La Libertad coast. Can combine with a beach day at Playa San Diego. Ranch + beach in one trip — strong logistics efficiency.

summary: Public contact lead in section: Surf.
why_text: Useful for booking, confirmations, and local coordination.

title: Barra de Santiago mangrove birding by kayak at dusk
summary: Sunset mangroves with seasonal bird concentration.
source_hint: Book eco guides in Barra de Santiago
why_text: Quiet, cinematic, and wildlife-rich.

title: AMBAS (Barra de Santiago community hatchery partner)
summary: Public contact lead in section: Turtles / Coastal nature.
why_text: Useful for booking, confirmations, and local coordination.

summary: Public contact lead in section: General tour operator backup.

summary: Public contact lead in section: Turtles / Coastal nature.
why_text: Useful for booking, confirmations, and local coordination.

title: My First Bitcoin (Mi Primer Bitcoin)
summary: Salvadoran bitcoin education nonprofit with public bitcoin donation checkout.
why_text: Supports open-source bitcoin education and teacher training based in El Salvador.

title: PDX Customer Service (Portland International Airport)
summary: Public contact lead in section: Flights / airport operations (Step 1 reliability contacts).
why_text: Useful for booking, confirmations, and local coordination.

title: Private shore snorkel and freedive intro
summary: Small-group skill day booked through local dive shops.
why_text: Hands-on water progression, not just passive sightseeing.

title: TSA Contact Center (for checkpoint/security process support)
summary: Public contact lead in section: Flights / airport operations (Step 1 reliability contacts).
why_text: Useful for booking, confirmations, and local coordination.

title: Los Cobanos Marine-Fauna Expedition (Protected Area)
summary: Two-hour small-boat marine-fauna exploration in the Los Cobanos protected area with recurring sightings of fish, dolphins, and turtles.
source_hint: El Salvador Turismo marine-fauna itinerary (2-hour skiff expedition in Los Cobanos protected area)
why_text: Adds a lighter water-nature option for non-anglers while keeping marine biodiversity exposure high.

title: Entrecafé Experience — Coffee Farm + Glamping (Los Naranjos)
summary: Agrotourism coffee farm at Finca Los Naranjos, Juayúa. Hands-on specialty coffee experience, nature hikes, camping and glamping accommodations. Multiple SV coffee varietals on-site.
why_text: Best coffee agrotourism near Juayúa. Full farm experience — from plant to cup. Glamping option makes it overnight-capable. Strong local people + food signal. Juayúa is a weekend food market town — pairs perfectly.

title: Hacienda La Carrera — Cacao Farm Tour (Usulután)
summary: Largest cacao plantation in El Salvador — 350 hectares in Usulután. 125-year-old family hacienda run by Juan Wright. Grows Trinitario cacao, coffee, and timber with on-site fermentation and drying facilities.
why_text: El Salvador's premier cacao estate. Award-winning Trinitario beans exported to European craft chocolate makers. Combines agro-tourism, food production heritage, and possible farm walk. Strong offbeat + local_people signal. 90min from San Salvador near Jiquilisco Bay (can pair with bay wildlife day).

title: Hismar Sanchez Ranch (Lourdes, La Libertad)
summary: Top-ranked El Salvador ranch on Tripadvisor. Located in Lourdes, La Libertad. Open Mon–Fri and Sunday 8am–8:30pm. Authentic Salvadoran ranch experience close to San Salvador.
why_text: #1 ranked ranch in El Salvador on Tripadvisor. Very close to San Salvador base (La Libertad dept). Strong local culture signal. Easy half-day or evening trip. Lourdes is ~30min from San Salvador center.

title: Barra de Santiago AMBAS Hatchery Support and Neonate Release
summary: Community-run hatchery support session in the Barra de Santiago Ramsar zone with possible neonate release activities tied to local conservation teams.
source_hint: FIAES Quelonia update on Barra de Santiago + AMBAS conservation execution
why_text: Strengthens conservation authenticity by anchoring to a documented local partner with quantified hatchery and release outcomes.

title: Playa Esteron Turtle Sanctuary Hatchling Release (La Tortuga Verde)
summary: Evening conservation briefing and potential hatchling release window at Playa Esteron with a turtle-sanctuary operator in Intipuca.
source_hint: La Tortuga Verde site + Contact page (turtle sanctuary description and active phone/WhatsApp contact)
why_text: Adds a direct-contact turtle option on the eastern coast with explicit sanctuary context and practical WhatsApp-first coordination.

title: Ruta de la Tortuga Marina Interpretive Hatchery Circuit
summary: One-day interpretive visit to a selected hatchery on El Salvador's turtle route, with conservation talks and seasonal release/watch opportunities.
source_hint: MARN Ruta de la Tortuga Marina (25 hatcheries, symbolic visit cost, release/watch windows)
why_text: Adds a structured, policy-backed turtle option that can be tuned by region and trip logistics instead of relying on one beach only.

title: Friends of Jiquilisco Bay (conservation contact)
summary: Public contact lead in section: Monkeys / Bay nature.
why_text: Useful for booking, confirmations, and local coordination.

title: Puerto Barillas (Jiquilisco Bay)
summary: Public contact lead in section: Monkeys / Bay nature.
why_text: Useful for booking, confirmations, and local coordination.

title: Los Cobanos Half-Day Trolling Charter with Local Captain
summary: Half-day (8:00-13:00) trolling trip from Los Cobanos bay for up to four people with local captain support and supplied equipment.
source_hint: El Salvador Divers Other Activities (half-day fishing) + Fishing Tours El Salvador booking fallback
why_text: Adds a time-bounded fish-first option that fits a coastal base day without consuming the full itinerary.

title: Cooperativa Los Pinos — Coffee + Cabin Stay (Lake Coatepeque)
summary: Coffee farm near Lake Coatepeque specializing in Bourbon beans, with cabin accommodations and farm tours.
source_hint: Journeyable eco coffee tours El Salvador
why_text: Combines coffee farm experience, accommodation, and lake access. Bourbon varietal focus. Local cooperative — strong local_people signal.

title: Coatepeque Lake boat and restaurant loop
summary: Volcanic lake swim and relaxed lakeside dining.
why_text: Great recharge day between activity spikes.

title: Laguna El Jocotal Sunrise Wetland Birding by Boat
summary: First-light small-boat birding circuit across one of El Salvador's key wetland habitats for herons, waterfowl, and migratory shorebirds.
source_hint: Ramsar Site Information Service + local boat/guide coordination
why_text: Dawn timing improves sightings and comfort, while wetland habitat adds high biodiversity value beyond surf-coast activities.

title: Parque del Bicentenario Turquoise-browed Motmot Stop
summary: Quick San Salvador stop with documented views of the national bird, the Turquoise-browed Motmot, near Ecoparque El Espino.
source_hint: The Speckled Hatchback birding trip report (2018)
why_text: Useful urban-adjacent birding add-on when staying in San Salvador. A published birding trip report specifically noted a strong Turquoise-browed Motmot sighting here after birding Ecoparque El Espino.

title: Cacao Tour El Salvador (San Julián, Sonsonate)
summary: Hands-on cacao farm experience at Finca Cristo Negro, San Julián (km 16.5 road to Cuisnahuat). Chocolate craft workshop, Mayan cacao history, bean-to-bar tasting. Instagram @cacaotour.
why_text: Best-reviewed organized cacao tour in El Salvador per Tripadvisor. Near Juayúa and the Ruta de las Flores — easy to pair with a western highlands day. Authentic small-farm experience.

title: Night-time turtle watch with conservation groups
summary: Seasonal nesting patrol with permits and legal constraints.
why_text: One of the most meaningful wildlife encounters if in season.

title: Balboa Park MotMot Forest-edge Session
summary: Forest-edge birding on the Panchimalco side of San Salvador with both Turquoise-browed Motmot and Blue-crowned Motmot listed as notable species.
source_hint: Birding El Salvador hot spots guide
why_text: Strong accessible birding option close to the capital when you want a realistic shot at MotMots without a full-day mission. Birding El Salvador lists both Turquoise-browed and Blue-crowned Motmot here.

title: Finca San Luis — Horseback Riding & Rainforest Trails
summary: Working ranch SW of San Salvador with ~30 horses, rainforest trail rides, and rural countryside. Popular with locals for day escapes.
source_hint: Horseback riding El Salvador near San Salvador
why_text: Large horse operation with rainforest trails close to San Salvador. ~30 horses means group rides possible. SW of city — easy day trip direction. Strong wildlife + agro-tourism combo.

title: Cinquera Rainforest Park National Bird Loop
summary: Birding-friendly forest park and history stop where outside travel coverage explicitly calls out the Turquoise-browed Motmot as a highlight.
why_text: Useful if you want the national bird in a less urban, more story-rich setting. AFAR explicitly notes Cinquera Rainforest Park as home to the Turquoise-browed Motmot.

title: Ilopango Skin-Diving and Snorkel Skills Day
summary: Freshwater snorkel and skin-diving session at Lake Ilopango with optional PADI Skin Diver structure and beginner refresh support.
source_hint: El Salvador Divers General Dives + Dive Sites (Ilopango all year round; snorkel + PADI Skin Diver)
why_text: Covers the snorkel/freedive-style requirement near San Salvador with lower transfer friction than coast-only sessions.

title: El Imposible National Park half-day hike
summary: Steep forest trails with endemic birds and rugged views.
why_text: Best inland biodiversity hit near your west route.

title: Birdwatch micro-tour at Los Naranjos/private fincas
summary: Targeted endemic species dawn sessions.
why_text: High birding precision and low crowd exposure.

title: Botanical and butterfly gardens around Santa Tecla
summary: Easy-access slow nature day close to city services.
source_hint: Garden sites and local volunteer options
why_text: Low-friction recovery day with biodiversity.

title: Lake Ilopango Dawn Dry-Forest Birding and Caldera Descent
summary: Early birding walk from dry-forest slopes to Ilopango shoreline with a 4WD-assisted return to avoid uphill heat exposure.
